page: Microsoft Azure E8ns_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Env6 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and 8-bit integer operations. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include local storage, requiring external premium storage volumes for data persistence. It is designated as network-optimized and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data shows average performance across most standard compute and memory tasks. The stress-ng single-core score is 1917.38, and the multi-core score is 7845.77. Memory bandwidth is measured at 608.18 GB/sec for small cached blocks, dropping to 61.47 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 42.33 GB/sec for large uncached writes. Single-threaded gzip compression demonstrates strong performance at 30.74 GB/sec, while bzip3 decompression reaches 13.32 GB/sec single-threaded and 51.08 GB/sec multi-threaded. In database operations, the server achieves a PassMark database score of 4759.95 and processes 10.8 million Redis SET operations per second.

For artificial intelligence workloads, the hardware supports AMX instructions, enabling a strong text generation speed of 311.01 tokens/second on a small 135M parameter LLM. However, performance drops to a weak 8.17 tokens/second for text generation on a medium 7B model. Given the 64.0 GB memory capacity and network-optimized design, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and network-heavy applications that do not require local NVMe storage or heavy GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E8ns_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Env6 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, supported by 64.0 GB of RAM, which provides a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The processor includes a large 260 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. This server lacks local storage, relying instead on premium remote storage, and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core compute and memory bandwidth, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression at 30.74 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong text generation speeds of 311.01 tokens/second for small LLMs, though performance degrades on larger models. This configuration is suited for database operations, memory-intensive applications, and network-optimized workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8ns_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for database and network-optimized workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8ns_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and premium storage support.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, advanced matrix extensions, and high memory-to-core ratios for database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
No local storage, requiring premium remote storage volumes
Strong single-threaded gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation
Suited for database operations and network-optimized workloads
